title: IReactorClient is a high-level Promise-based wrapper while IReactor provides low-level fire-and-forget job submission
content: The `IReactorClient` interface wraps `IReactor` to provide a simpler Promise-based API. Key differences: IReactorClient write methods wait for `READ_READY` status before resolving (returning final documents), while IReactor returns `JobInfo` immediately. IReactorClient manages signing internally and handles consistency tokens automatically; IReactor requires explicit `ISigner` and manual token passing. IReactorClient provides convenience methods like `createEmpty()`, `createDocumentInDrive()`, `rename()`, `moveChildren()`, and `deleteDocuments()`. IReactorClient offers `subscribe(search, callback)` for real-time changes; IReactor requires direct event bus access. Use IReactor when you need fire-and-forget jobs, explicit consistency token control, custom signing, or `executeBatch()` for multi-document atomic operations.
description: IReactorClient waits for job completion and returns documents; IReactor returns JobInfo immediately for fire-and-forget patterns.
